what: First ON-RING --seed-shards proof: a joiner pulled its verified layer range from RINGMATES over the shard DHT while the ring SERVED. Closes the torrent loop #61 left owed (WAN peer-fetch was proven point-to-point; this is the production shape: every stage's sidecar seeds its verified range from the same tunnel daemon, joiner pulls peers-first, mirror only as fallback).

ring.sidecar: HEAD build incl. PR #68 same-peer offset-resume; -seed manifest=/root/m25 per stage, DHT bootstrap via predecessor

joiner.box: 44342193 (24GB 4090, NL-listed, ~72ms real RTT to the pool)
joiner.result: rc=0, 9 files / 28.6 GB verified into /root/m25_join, 2394 s (~12 MB/s avg single-stream WAN)
joiner.sources: 6 of 7 weight shards + all config files from RINGMATES (NO alone SERVED 3 complete shards); 1 shard (model-00010) fell back to the mirror after progress-gated retries
joiner.trust: every byte re-hashed vs the signed manifest per source, exactly as the mirror path

fix_this_run_forced: First attempt (pre-resume sidecar): the seeder sent 4.27/5.0 GB, one 60 s WAN stall discarded the partial and forced mirror fallback on shard ONE. PR #68 (same-peer offset-resume, progress-gated retries) shipped mid-session and pushed to all boxes; the rerun sourced 6/7 shards P2P.
notes: Serving and seeding co-existed: the batched driver ran its full pass on the SAME warm ring during seeding. Remaining known gap: the k=0 (head) sidecar's initial DHT provide fires before the mesh forms ('failed to find any peer in table') — direct-dial fallback covers joiners bootstrapped through it, but a periodic re-provide would make head-held ranges discoverable via the DHT proper.
